title: Whos The Boss

jokes.text: Behind every successful man, there's a woman. But if you wanted to switch positions, I'm with that too, know what I mean?
jokes.setup: Will starts with a conventional saying about successful men and women
jokes.punchline: Pivots to a sexual innuendo about 'switching positions'
jokes.explanation: Classic Will Smith charm - takes a wholesome saying and gives it his signature spin. Well-structured misdirection with strong character voice.

jokes.text: You're not gonna let a silly little thing like profits stand in the way of my promotion, are you?
jokes.setup: Professor criticizes Will for throwing parties instead of focusing on sales
jokes.punchline: Will dismisses profits as a 'silly little thing' for a business manager
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Will's street-smart confidence clashing with business reality. Great ironic understatement that reveals his priorities.

jokes.text: Very meticulous. Bakes me fresh muffins every day. No. No. No, he's not:
jokes.setup: Professor describes the ideal candidate who bakes muffins
jokes.punchline: Will's horrified realization it's Carlton, delivered in fragmented denial
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup-payoff with Will's dawning horror. The fragmented 'No' delivery suggests great physical comedy from Will Smith.

jokes.text: Get to the insulting part. 'Hilary Banks doesn't know the difference between a cirrus cloud and a cumulus cloud.' What is the difference? Will, look around. Do you see my TelePrompTer here?
jokes.setup: Hilary reads criticism about not knowing cloud types
jokes.punchline: Immediately proves the point by not knowing the difference herself
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Hilary proves her critics right in the act of defending herself. Perfect ironic structure.

jokes.text: I don't know. Let's see. Short, obnoxious. All you need is a bad haircut. And your ears.
jokes.setup: Carlton compares himself to Ross Perot
jokes.punchline: Will lists similarities, adding 'your ears' as cruel escalation
jokes.explanation: Classic cousin roasting with good escalation structure. The 'and your ears' addition shows Will's mean streak.

jokes.text: Fruity Pebbles! And there's a little tugboat inside, Ross.
jokes.setup: Carlton just bragged about his maturity and business savvy
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by cereal like a child
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contradiction joke. Carlton's childlike excitement immediately undercuts his business pretensions. Great juxtaposition.

jokes.text: How do they feel about guys who still depend on the crossing guard? Hey, that's a dangerous intersection, mister.
jokes.setup: Will mocks Carlton's maturity
jokes.punchline: Carlton earnestly defends using crossing guards as adult
jokes.explanation: Great character beat showing Carlton's sheltered upbringing. His earnest defense makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Especially Master William who will discover the joy of working for a Banks.
jokes.setup: Philip says this will be good experience for both boys
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's dry observation about Will working for the family
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey dry wit. Subtle class commentary delivered with perfect butler deadpan.

jokes.text: Oh, well, you know, sort of like your lectures. Long, stale and full of baloney.
jokes.setup: Professor asks how the subs are today
jokes.punchline: Will compares subs to professor's lectures with triple insult
jokes.explanation: Solid setup-punchline structure with good wordplay connecting literal and figurative 'baloney'. Will's confidence selling.

jokes.text: I'm stupid. I just failed, didn't I? Look, check it out, here's a sub on the house. Instant A.
jokes.setup: Professor realizes he just got insulted
jokes.punchline: Will smoothly offers free food to fix grades
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Will's street smarts and charm. Quick thinking to turn insult into opportunity.

jokes.text: So she'll see me again? Oh, no, man, she hates your guts. Hey, but look, there's always more fish in the sea. I should know. They call me Aquaman.
jokes.setup: Will builds up Dexter's hopes about his ex
jokes.punchline: Crushes hopes, then makes bizarre Aquaman boast
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection followed by absurd self-aggrandizement. The Aquaman reference is perfectly random and confident.

jokes.text: That's fly, ain't it? I like to think of it as my own Sistine Chapel.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains about Jet magazine beauties on ceiling
jokes.punchline: Will compares his soft-core collage to Michelangelo's masterpiece
jokes.explanation: Brilliant high-low culture collision. Will's proud comparison of pin-ups to Renaissance art is perfectly absurd and character-specific.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, Phillipe's been in there worshipping for two hours.
jokes.setup: Continues the Sistine Chapel metaphor
jokes.punchline: Student literally 'worshipping' the pin-ups for hours
jokes.explanation: Good follow-through on the religious art metaphor. Visual gag of student staring at ceiling for hours.

jokes.text: My name's Will. Welcome to The Peacock, where our motto is: Girl, whatever you got, I want it.
jokes.setup: Carlton gives Will specific corporate greeting to recite
jokes.punchline: Will flips 'whatever you want, we've got it' into pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfect character subversion. Will takes corporate speak and makes it his own with clever wordplay reversal.

jokes.text: Will, you're getting it all wrong. Hey, look, man, as long as I'm getting it.
jokes.setup: Carlton criticizes Will's performance
jokes.punchline: Will turns 'getting it wrong' into sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Typical Will sexual wordplay. Quick and in-character but not particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: Yes, sir, General Shorcoff.
jokes.setup: Carlton asserts authority as boss
jokes.punchline: Will mocks him with 'Short-off' military title pun
jokes.explanation: Clever height-based pun disguised as military respect. Shows Will's ability to mock while appearing compliant.

jokes.text: Can't you just throw them in jail or something?
jokes.setup: Hilary asks her judge father for help with critics
jokes.punchline: Assumes judicial power can jail newsletter writers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - privileged and naive about how justice works. Shows her entitled worldview.

jokes.text: Did you really refer to Hurricane Robert as Bobby? Well, Robert just sounded so serious. It was serious, darling. It wiped out half of Miami. Well, excuse me for trying to spread a little sunshine.
jokes.setup: Philip questions Hilary's hurricane nickname
jokes.punchline: Hilary defends making devastating hurricane sound cute
jokes.explanation: Excellent character-driven irony. Hilary's clueless optimism in face of natural disaster is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: Someone who knows what it's like to be hated. 555-9021. Oh, Shannen? Hi. Hilary Banks. You remember that I Hate Brenda thing that you went through? What do you mean you don't know me? You spilled a drink on me at Roxbury. Hello? Boy, she's not gonna make many friends with that attitude.
jokes.setup: Hilary calls someone who understands being hated
jokes.punchline: Calls Shannen Doherty, gets hung up on, misses irony
jokes.explanation: Great meta-reference to 90s pop culture and Shannen Doherty's reputation. Hilary's obliviousness to the irony makes it perfect.

jokes.text: What do you mean you don't know me? You spilled a drink on me at Roxbury. Hello?
jokes.setup: Hilary calls Shannen Doherty for advice about being hated
jokes.punchline: Shannen hangs up on her, Hilary thinks it's attitude problem
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Hilary's delusions about her celebrity status

jokes.text: Look, I particularly recommend the shrimpy cocktail.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to upsell customers
jokes.punchline: Says 'shrimpy' instead of 'shrimp' cocktail
jokes.explanation: Minor malapropism that fits Carlton's pretentious but imperfect speech patterns. Adequate but not memorable.

jokes.text: Carlton, I think we need to have a little talk, man. You know, bird to nerd.
jokes.setup: Will wants to have serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'bird to bird' with Carlton as nerd
jokes.explanation: Clever phrase twist that perfectly captures their dynamic. Will's casual cruelty to Carlton in wordplay form.

jokes.text: So, ladies, have you decided? Drop dead. Excellent choice.
jokes.setup: Carlton asks hostile customers for their order
jokes.punchline: Treats 'drop dead' as menu selection
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response showing Carlton's cluelessness. Great economy and timing, treats insult as customer service.

jokes.text: We've got these people by the textbooks. We can charge and pretty much do whatever we want. This is like sex to you, isn't it? Yes.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains his monopolistic business philosophy
jokes.punchline: Carlton admits business manipulation sexually excites him
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character revelation - Carlton's ruthless capitalism as sexual thrill. His honest 'Yes' makes it perfect.

jokes.text: You're just some butt-kissing, apple-a-day suck up that got lucky.
jokes.setup: Will finally explodes at Carlton's authority
jokes.punchline: Strings together multiple teacher's pet insults
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's frustration. Multiple insults work well together, though individually predictable.

jokes.text: Count your blessings.
jokes.setup: Carlton complains Will won't talk to him
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey suggests this is actually good news
jokes.explanation: Classic Geoffrey understatement. Perfect economy but predictable butler snark.

jokes.text: Otherwise, I'd be out parasailing with these.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey explains need to work for money
jokes.punchline: Shows off something expensive (likely parasailing equipment)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Geoffrey's hidden interests. Visual gag loses impact in transcript.

jokes.text: Oh, damn. That mean they took down the Naughty Nightie girl.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil complains about anti-Hilary billboard
jokes.punchline: Will mourns loss of sexy advertisement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will priorities - cares more about sexy billboard than family drama. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: If I watch the weather in New York, I'll have a three-hour jump on the competition. Honey, that might not work every time.
jokes.setup: Hilary thinks she has brilliant weather strategy
jokes.punchline: Doesn't understand weather is location-specific
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - completely backwards understanding of how weather works. Philip's gentle correction adds to it.

jokes.text: Chance of snow and a minus three wind chill. Well, my work is done. I'm going shopping.
jokes.setup: Hilary forecasts freezing weather
jokes.punchline: Goes shopping despite predicting dangerous conditions
jokes.explanation: Good character irony - Hilary ignores her own weather advice. Shows her priorities and disconnect from reality.

jokes.text: Now, dress warm, it's gonna snow.
jokes.setup: Hilary was reporting New York weather earlier
jokes.punchline: Tells LA family to dress for snow based on NY forecast
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the time zone confusion - she's applying NY weather to LA

jokes.text: Also, we are pleased to announce that the proceeds from our new pay toilets have enabled us to buy this brand new metal detector.
jokes.setup: Carlton announces restaurant 'improvements'
jokes.punchline: Pay toilets fund security equipment for restaurant
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Carlton's terrible business decisions. Pay toilets in college restaurant is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Remember, less flatware theft means lower prices for you, our valued consumer.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains metal detector purpose
jokes.punchline: Claims anti-theft measures help customers while raising prices
jokes.explanation: Good corporate doublespeak parody. Carlton's business logic is backwards but delivered with confidence.

jokes.text: Peacock-a-cola? I made that one up myself. It shows.
jokes.setup: Will proudly presents his invented drink name
jokes.punchline: Jazz immediately recognizes it's amateur
jokes.explanation: Clean setup-punchline with Jazz deflating Will's pride. Good economy and timing.

jokes.text: Phillipe told me you cut the teeth marks off the pickles and reuse them.
jokes.setup: Jazz lists Carlton's cost-cutting measures
jokes.punchline: Reveals disgustingly unsanitary pickle recycling
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly disgusting detail that perfectly captures Carlton's penny-pinching taken to absurd extreme. Original and memorable.

jokes.text: My babies. Even Oliver? He has a heart murmur. Even Oliver.
jokes.setup: Carlton offers to pay Jazz's pigeon vet bills
jokes.punchline: Jazz emotionally mentions sick pigeon by name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jazz character moment - treating pigeons like children. The specific medical condition makes it funnier.

jokes.text: If anybody asks, I've been with you all day.
jokes.setup: Professor sees the bizarre crowd Jazz brought
jokes.punchline: Immediately wants alibi for association
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to visual chaos. Professor's immediate damage control is relatable and well-timed.

jokes.text: Who is this Bosnia person I keep hearing about?
jokes.setup: Hilary gets promoted to anchorperson
jokes.punchline: Thinks Bosnia is a person, not a country
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary ignorance of world events. Treating war-torn country as celebrity name is perfectly in-character.

jokes.text: Will, you can't be happy here. This is a franchise. The kind of place where they treat employees like faceless, anonymous nobodies.
jokes.setup: Carlton criticizes franchise employee treatment
jokes.punchline: Sets up for immediate contradiction of his point
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the employee recognition that follows. Carlton's criticism creates perfect ironic contrast.

jokes.text: Will Smith, come on up here. I know you've only been here four days, but the staff has voted you employee of the week!
jokes.setup: Carlton just criticized franchise employee treatment
jokes.punchline: Will immediately gets honored as employee of the week
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and irony. Carlton's point gets demolished immediately by reality. Great structure.

jokes.text: Oh, Carlton, save me from this hellhole.
jokes.setup: Will just got honored and praised
jokes.punchline: Sarcastically begs to be 'saved' from good treatment
jokes.explanation: Great sarcastic delivery that twists the knife in Carlton's failed argument. Perfect character smugness.

jokes.text: All those summers at business camp were just a waste of time.
jokes.setup: Carlton admits defeat
jokes.punchline: Reveals he went to business camp as child
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton detail - business camp instead of regular summer camp. Shows his sheltered, business-focused upbringing.

jokes.text: But together we'd smell like success.
jokes.setup: Will says they both 'stink' individually
jokes.punchline: Continues smell metaphor with 'smell like success'
jokes.explanation: Clever continuation of the smell metaphor. Good wordplay that maintains the partnership theme.

jokes.text: Beg? I don't know how to beg. I'm rich.
jokes.setup: Will wants Carlton to beg for his return
jokes.punchline: Carlton's privilege means he's never had to beg
jokes.explanation: Perfect class-conscious humor. Carlton's honest assessment of his privilege is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: Look, a man's most valuable possession is his self-respect. Make it 2000. All right, let me go get my hat.
jokes.setup: Will gives noble speech about self-respect over money
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons principles when price doubles
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection showing Will's pragmatic nature trumping his principles. Perfect comedic structure and timing.

tmdb_overview: Carlton in charge. Will gets his feathers ruffled when his cousin becomes his boss at the Peacock Stop.
